name: Old-Fashioned Butterscotch Lollipops
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Country Home, Holidays at Home 1994
1. Line several baking sheets with waxed paper and set aside.
2. Grease the sides of a heavy 1-quart saucepan with butter.
3. In the saucepan, combine sugar, corn syrup, water, and vinegar. Cook over medium-high heat for about 5 minutes until boiling, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon to dissolve the sugar. Avoid splashing the mixture on the sides of the pan.
4. Carefully attach a candy thermometer to the side of the saucepan.
5. Continue cooking the mixture over medium heat, stirring constantly, while adding butter in 2 pieces at a time.
6. Cook until the thermometer reaches 300 degrees (hard-crack stage). The candy mixture should boil at a moderate, steady rate over the entire surface. Reaching hard-crack stage may take 25 to 30 minutes.
7. Remove the saucepan from the heat and detach the thermometer. Stir in vanilla extract.
8. Allow cooling for 5 minutes, then pour the mixture onto the prepared baking sheets. It will form 2-inch circles.
9. Quickly place a lollipop stick into each piece of candy, twisting gently to cover with the candy mixture.
10. Let the lollipops harden and wrap them individually in clear plastic wrap to store at room temperature. Makes 12-16 lollipops.
